学校简直令人难以置信。过去一个月在网上学校上学，很容易忘记自己的学校状况有多糟糕。
 在网上学校，他们负责您的关注。您不必选择进入大脑的内容。但是，您可以静音，并具有一般的自治权，可以快速完成工作并转移到更重要的事情上。
  在实际的学校中，他们可以控制您的身体。在你的眼里。在您的注意力的各个方面以及进入大脑的所有内容，您都无法选择静音，因为听着瞧我们的耳朵永远不会让我们停止听力，老师也不会停止喊叫。
  在2020年的最后几个月里，我对学校的记忆犹新，这真是太疯狂了，花了这么多时间从老师的网站上乱扔论文，以至于我不会忘记那些浮现在脑海的想法！
 如果不是老师希望您看到的论文，则论文是非法的！
   《敌人的真面目》是此网站上的一则帖子，其中有一个代表学校系统的类恶魔吹嘘它在逃离学校系统的道路上（特别是对儿童而言）设置的许多许多危害。
 我将其发布在LessWrong上，几乎所有读者都忽略了这一点。他们说，“学校实际上不是怪物”。 ‘我对这种言辞感到生气-它对油性皮肤的学校有何评价？” 
我十五岁，《敌人的真实面孔》中详述的怪物恰恰是学校对我的感觉。
 我今年15岁，在2020年的最后四个月里，我为逃避学校系统而无休止地跨越着无尽的障碍-为了我的理智，健康和睡眠，我从未被允许过。
 一次又一次地告诉我‘你应该完成学业！您就快快完了！您几乎“做到了”！哎呀，当我上大学时，我必须坚持到底。
 不！不！不要将您选择的目标与强迫我的目标进行比较！
 我什么时候被告知：“您可以在接下来的五年里上学，这可能会稍微增加您就业或上另一所与此类型大致相同的学校的机会，但是这会因您承受巨大的压力和周围的环境而致富充满愤怒，不快乐和匆忙的气氛。而且，我们将助您一臂之力。
  哦，如果您不服从任何一个，我们可以随时带您进入一个房间，并且：a）只要我们想要，您就向您尖叫b）将您放到另一个房间，您不能与其他人讲话，必须在一天的剩余时间里不断写作。
  哦，如果您之后改变主意，它将被忽略。” 
不，我没有这个选择。我11岁那年，这所学校被逼给我。我现在是否在为自己11岁的不为在家上学而奋斗的人买单？那公平吗？那有意义吗？
    不。如果您不去上班，您的老板会来敲门吗？要求您站在他的面前，压倒您的求职信，先去买口罩，然后将您分流到出租车上（因为他当您走到那里时，脸上会冒出烟气（我没有化妆）），将您送入办公室并与另一个负责人组队，对着您大喊20分钟，而忽略了您的无罪诉求？
 不。如果您不工作，就会丢掉工作，也得不到钱。但是您永远不会被迫工作。
 工作是自下而上的结构。您会受到激励去上班，但没有被强迫。
 这打开了市场。如果您确定自己有一个更好的赚钱的主意，则可以自由尝试-导致成千上万的惊人业务和自谋职业的惊人流行。
 学校是自上而下的结构。你被迫上学。手段（学校教育）比目标（学习）要困难得多，所以当200年过去了，并且有更好的方法可以达到目标，而没有以前手段的所有麻烦，而不是简单地退出学校并尝试自己，你必须跳跨栏跨栏，跨栏。仍然老师来敲你的门。
 “穿衣服，”他说。 “我带你去学校。” 
我很有意识，我还活着，我有很多想法-就像你一样。
 在社交场合中，当脸部模糊不清且仅凭我的思想和言语才能对我进行判断时，我一遍又一遍地被告知，我与年龄远大于我的人没有区别。
  我可以在某处进行测试吗？在某个地方，我用手指指着，然后写着“ BEEP BEEP BEEP，此用户有意识，请给他选择命运的权利。”
 我是否可以在某个地方上载此博客的全部内容，并且可以负责我自己，我从事的工作和去向，而实际上可以代替别人吗？
 或者（并且，请告诉我，情况并非如此，因为我们俩都知道那绝对是疯了）实际上是唯一可以衡量其健康和成年18年的理智和成年的方法。当然不是吧？
   最糟糕的是，我很反常！如果有一项测试可以测试您的智力以确定您是否应享有自由，那么我会通过，因为多年来我一直在这种环境和影响下发疯，因为我可以利用这些知识使智力蓬勃发展，因为以及（有人告诉我）出生时的一些原始情报。
 翻阅gingerjumble档案，您会发现我每天养成的最佳习惯的证据，那就是每天阅读。从理论上讲，这保持了我的学习动力，这意味着当我在2020年中期被介绍给SuperMemo时，我能够使用它来快速学习和享受它，而我现在就在这里。 
……与成为一个无趣的人相反，它没有叛逆或鼓动去改变事物，并且在沮丧的边缘时刻不断地在叙述-他们的叙述不足以使他们决定去哪里或去看什么。根深蒂固的是他们所习得的无助的形象。相对于让老师塞满他们不感兴趣的知识的船只而言，直到他们被无用的废话充斥着边缘之时，他们才没有容纳任何东西的空间，这种能力永远消失了。
 哈哈，很好，我很好，对吧？从理论上讲，我是通过了一项像成人测试一样理智的测试，而不是通过这些可怜的灵魂，显然谁也不会通过？
   与这些对生命充满热情的可怜的灵魂相反，他们对鼓励叛乱和逃避现实的事物几乎没有兴趣，他们的脚牢牢地扎根在暴政和服从的真实教育世界中，他们对生活的热爱-对于任何事物-永远迷路了吗？
    如果您告诉孩子一生要做什么，那么它将无法自律。
 如果您告诉孩子一生吃什么，那将无法区分什么是健康的。
 如果您告诉孩子一生要学习什么，它将无法区分哪些知识是有用的。
 如果在异国星球上，甚至有机会证明孩子们有意识的孩子，而您在学校学习十年后对他们进行了测量，他们都会失败！ 
这不是因为他们不值得自由，而是因为他们从未自由！您已经将它们与您的旧系统结合使用，它们不再能够证明自己的价值！
  那我为什么在这里？并自觉地对生活和真正的学习充满了爱和欣赏？
 因为我很幸运。我真是太幸运了，我讨厌它。因为我所有的朋友都不那么幸运，也因为他们热情的自我被谋杀了。
  以为我夸张了吗？也许您已经足够远离学校的界限了，想起看到生活中的爱情从朋友面前流失的感觉是什么样的，因为多年来的每一次对话都从激情和乐趣变成无尽的压力。
 也许您已经忘记看到微笑变得稀有的感觉了。
 或说你自己是学校压力僵尸之一。我不怪你也许您对自己遭受的苦难太深了，却对朋友的苦难视而不见。
  我上学的第八年是我一生中最糟糕的一年，因为我被上学的恐惧所惊醒，而且没有人可以分享它。 
在我无法逃脱的每一门数学课上，我都受到了伤害-拴在椅子上2个小时，而我所希望的只是起床，骑自行车并体验真实的世界。
 而且我很孤独-我还没有找到一个完全了解学业多么糟糕的人。
 当一个孩子被告知学校一生的好事而他们的学习不好时，他们会认为这是他们的错。这显然会破坏他们的自尊心。
   我清醒了，僵化了一年。我环顾四周，每个人都是僵尸，我感到恐惧，遭受了伤害，痛苦了一年。没有人像我一样-我还没有找到supermemo.guru。 ‘如果你不能击败他们，那就加入他们。’
  现在我又自由了，又重新僵化了，但是要走多久？我对结束学校奴役的热情转瞬即逝，记忆是如此的徒劳，而且很容易忘记被拖入无法逃脱的房间的巨大恐惧。
 多久以后，学校的危险才完全从我的脑海中消失，我变得和其他所有人一样，对学校的酷刑无动于衷，甚至坚持认为这是好事，尽管这造成了明显的，无法忍受的痛苦？
note_en:
School is unbelievably terrifying. Having been in online school for the past month, it’s easy to forget just how  bad in person school is.
 In online school, they are in charge of your attention. You don’t get to choose what goes into your brain. You are able to mute, though, And have general autonomy to do the work quickly and move onto more important things.
  In actual school, they have control over your very  body. Over your very  eyes. Over every aspect of your attention and what enters your brain, with no option to mute, because lo and behold our ears don’t ever let us stop hearing, and teachers never stop shouting.
  Running over my memories of school for the last few months of 2020, it’s absolutely  insane just how much of that time is spent scrambling papers out of teacher’s site just so that I don’t forget the ideas that come into my head!
 Paper is illegal if it’s not the papers the teachers want you to see!
   The True Face of the Enemy is a post on this site where a demon-like-being that represents the school system brags about the many, many hazards it’s set up on the path to escaping the school system, particularly for a child.
 I posted it on LessWrong, and the point was very much missed by almost all readers. ‘School isn’t actually a monster’, they said. ‘I’m offended at the rhetoric — what does it say about school that it has oily skin?’.
  I am fifteen, and the monster detailed in The True Face of the Enemy is excactly how school feels to me.
 I am fifteen, and I spent the last four months of 2020 jumping endless, endless hurdles trying to escape the school system — for my sanity and my health, and for the sleep I was never granted.
 I was told again and again and again and again that ‘you should finish school! You’re right near the end! You’ve almost “made it”! Heck, when I was in University I had to stick through to the end’
 NO! No! Do not compare the goals  you chose to the ones that were forced apon me!
 When was I ever told “You can go to school for next five years which may slightly increase your chance of getting employed or going onto another type of school which is mostly the same to this, at the cost of your wellbeing through intense stress and surrounded by a general aura of anger, unhappiness and hurry. And we’ll screw your sleep.
  Oh and if you disobey any of them we’re allowed to take you into a room at any point and: a) scream at you for as long as we want b)  put you in another room where you cannot speak to anyone else and must be constantly writing for the rest of the day.
  Oh and if you change your mind at any point after this it will be ignored.”
 No. I was not given that choice. This school was forced apon me when I was 11. Am I paying now for my eleven year old self not fighting to be home-schooled? Is that fair? Does that make sense?
    No. If you don’t go to work, does your employer come knocking apon your door, demanding you come stand right in front of him, overriding your pleadings to go and get a face mask first, shunting you into a cab (as he vapes in your face as you walk there [am not making this up]), shuttle you into an office and team up with another one in charge and shout at you for 20 minutes, ignoring your pleas of innocence?
 No. If you don’t work, you lose your job, and you get no money. But you are never  forced to work.
 Work is a bottom-up structure. You are incentivised to go to work, but not forced.
 This opens up the market. If you are sure you have a better idea of making money, you are free to try it — leading to thousands of amazing businesses and an wonderous epidemic of the self employed.
 School is a top-down structure. You are forced to go to school. The means (schooling) is worhipped more than the ends (learning) so when 200 years passed and there comes way better ways to reach the ends without all the hassles of the previous means, rather than simply quitting school and trying yourself, you must jump hurdle after hurdle after hurdle after hurdle. And still the teacher comes knocking on your door.
 “Get dressed,” he says. “I’m taking you to school.”
  I am concious, I am alive, I have a stream of thoughts — just like you.
 In social settings when faces are obfuscated and I am judged only on the merit of my thought and speech, I am told over and over and over that I am indistiguishable from people far older than me.
  Is there a test I can do somewhere? Someplace I stick my finger in and it goes ‘BEEP BEEP BEEP, this user is conscious, give him the rights to choose his fate.’
 Is there someplace I can upload the entirety of this blog, and in exchange  I can be incharge of me and what I do and where I go, instead of literally everybody else?
 Or (and please, please tell me this isn’t the case, becuase we both know that would be absolutely insane) is literally the only measure of sanity and adulthood actually being alive for a clean 18 years. Surely it’s not, right?
   The worst part is, I’m an anomaly! If there  were a test that tested your intelligence in order to determine if you deserved your freedom, I would pass because I’ve been insanely lucky over the years in the environments and influences I’ve had access to that allow intelligence to flourish, as well as (I’ve been told) some raw intelligence from birth.
 Scroll through the gingerjumble archives and you’ll see evidence of probably the best habit I ever formed, which was to read every day. I theorise this maintained my learn drive, meaning that when I was introduced to  SuperMemo in mid-2020 I was able to use it to learn rapidly and enjoy it, and here I am now.
 … As opposed to being an interest-less husk of a person, devoid of rebellion or muster to change things and at the very edge of depression constantly — the narrative that they are not smart enough to decide where to go or what to look at so ingrained into them they are the very images of learned helplessness. As opposed being a vessel for teachers to stuff knowledge they’re not interested into, until they are filled to the brim with useless nonsense they have no room for anything they enjoy, that capacity forever gone.
 Haha, good that I’m fine, right? That I’d theoretically pass this are-you-as-sane-as-an-adult-test, as opposed to these poor souls, who’d obviously never pass?
   As opposed to these poor souls who’s passion for life has been beaten in so thoroughly, with such little interest in things that encourage rebellion and escapism, their feet firmly planted in the real schooling world of tyranny and obedience that their love for life — for anything — is forever lost?
    If you tell a child what to do it’s entire life, it will not be able to govern itself.
 If you tell a child what to eat it’s entire life, it will not be able to distinguish what is healthy.
 If you tell a child what to learn it’s entire life, it will not be able to distinguish what knowledge is useful.
 If, on an alien planet where kids are even given the  chance to prove that they’re conscious, and you measure them  after ten years in school — they will all fail!
 And this is not because they are not worthy of freedom, but because they have never been free! You’ve broken them in with your archaic system, and they are no longer able to prove their worth!
  So why am I here? And concious, with some love and appreciation for life and true learning left?
 Because I got lucky. I got so so so so lucky and I hate it. Because all my friends were less lucky and because of that their passionate selves were murdered.
  Think I’m exaggerating? Perhaps you have been long enough away from the confines of school to remember what it’s like to see the love of life drain from the face of your friends, as every conversation over the course of years drifts from passions and enjoyments to endless stressors.
 Perhaps you’ve forgotten what it’s like to see smiles become rare.
 Or perhpas you were one of the school stress zombies yourself. I don’t blame you. Perhaps you were too deep in your own haze of suffering you were made blind to the suffering of your friends.
  My eighth year of schooling was the worst of my life, because I was awoken up to the horrors of schooling, and I had no one to share it with.
 I took damage in every maths class I couldn’t escape — chained to a chair for 2 hours when all I wished was to get up and cycle and experience the world for what it was.
 And I was lonely — I had not found anyone who quite understood how bad school was.
 When a child is told school is good all their lives and they do bad it in, they will assume it is their fault. And this will obviously wreck havock on their self esteem.
   I was awake, dezombified for a year. And I looked around me and everyone was a zombie, and I was terrified and I took damage and had a year in pain. No one else was like me — I hadn’t found  supermemo.guru yet. ‘If you can’t beat them, join them.’
  Now I am free and dezombified again, but for how long? My passion to end school slavery was fleeting, memories are so so futile and it’s so easy to forget the abject terror of being shuffled into a room  you cannot escape.
 How long until the perils of school flee entirely from my mind, and I become like everyone else, who are indifferent to the tortures of schooling — or even insist that it’s good — despite the obvious, unbearable misery it causes?
title: 我有意识，我正在伤害
title_en: I Am Conscious and I Am Hurting

支持该创业公司的天使投资人包括Matt Robinson（GoCardless的创始人），Paul Forester（Indeed的创始人）和Charlie Delingpole（ComplyAdvantage的创始人）。 TreeCard表示，这笔资金将用于雇用人才，支持其产品在英国的推广以及扩展到美国和“主要的欧洲市场”。
 为了成为“领先的绿色金融品牌”，TreeCard由Thiel研究员Jamie Cox（之前是Cashew的共同创立），Gary Wu和James Dugan于2020年8月成立。团队想出了将忠诚度积分或现金返还用于植树的想法，以期创建具有更多社会影响力的金融科技主张。
  注册后，您可以将TreeCard应用程序链接到您当前的银行帐户，以便开始通过由Mastercard支持的TreeCard路由支出。然后，您进行的购买（或具体来说是您的支出产生的一部分卡交易费用）将用于由绿色搜索引擎Ecosia（也是TreeCard的预种子投资者）运营的植树项目。
 “ [从高层次看，气候危机是人类在过去200,000年中面临的最大生存风险；我们认为，引导消费者资金流动是影响变革的最有力方法。”首席执行官考克斯（Cox）告诉我。 “我们正在建立一家金融公司，该公司不仅可以让消费者减少支出损失，还可以积极改善世界。
 “我们正在建立一个免费的消费卡，使消费者可以更负责任地消费。该卡使用交换来重新构建他们的支出，并使用复杂的分析来帮助他们确定健康的支出以及破坏性支出。”
 当然，与美国相比，英国/欧盟的消费者卡交换费用非常低。提供消费卡和帐户并非没有开销，因此，尚不清楚TreeCard仅凭交换收入就能实现可持续发展。也许不足为奇的是，在美国，产生费用较高的美国被视为初创企业的主要启动市场。
 考克斯解释说：“美国的互换费明显高于欧盟，因此这为我们提供了足够的收入机会来进行植树造林投资并支付营销和管理成本。” “在欧盟，我们将与现有的零售银行合作，后者将免费提供我们所有的银行基础设施。这意味着，即使我们的转乘费削减幅度较小，也足以支付我们在欧盟的费用。我们将在短期内宣布银行名称。” 
同时，TreeCard创始人将早期支持者Ecosia称为其“母公司”。 “他们是我们最紧密的合作伙伴，随着我们的成长，我们将与他们紧密合作，” Cox说。 “他们向公司投资了第一笔支票，并将为我们做所有的植树活动。 Ecosia的营销团队经验非常丰富，他们将帮助我们在未来几年内将搜索引擎用作获取用户的核心渠道。”  EQT Ventures的交易合作伙伴Tom Mendoza评论：“ TreeCard有潜力成为领先的绿色金融品牌，在为影响力的财务管理创建事实上的平台之前，没有其他品牌可以做到。 在殷拓创投，我们越来越意识到环境及其投资对我们周围世界的影响，因此我们非常高兴能够支持TreeCard团队，他们正在与金融系统积极合作，共同创造更美好的未来 为地球”。
note_en:
Angel investors also backing the startup include Matt Robinson (founder of GoCardless), Paul Forester (founder of Indeed) and Charlie Delingpole (founder of ComplyAdvantage). TreeCard says the funding will be used to hire talent, support the roll-out of its product across the U.K. and to expand into the U.S. and “key European markets”.
 Aiming to become a “leading green finance brand”, TreeCard was founded in August 2020 by Thiel fellow Jamie Cox (who previously co-founded Cashew), Gary Wu and James Dugan. The team hit onto the idea of swapping loyalty points or cash back for tree planting, in a bid to create a fintech proposition with more societal impact.
  Once signed up, you link the TreeCard app to your current bank accounts so you can begin routing your spending through the Mastercard-powered TreeCard. Purchases you then make — or, specifically, a portion of the card transaction fees your spending generates — is then put toward tree planting projects run by green search engine Ecosia, which is also a pre-seed investor in TreeCard.
 “[At a] high level, the climate crisis is the biggest existential risk that humanity has faced in the last 200,000 years; we believe directing the flow of consumer finances is the most powerful way to affect change,” CEO Cox tells me. “We’re building a finance company that allows consumers to not just to do less damage with their spending, but to actively improve the world.
 “We are building a free spending card that allows consumers to spend more responsibly. The card uses interchange to reforest as they spend and sophisticated analytics to help them identify healthy spending as well as destructive ones”.
 Of course, consumer card interchange fees in the U.K./EU are very low compared to the U.S. Offering a spending card and account isn’t without overhead, so it isn’t clear how sustainable TreeCard could be on interchange revenue alone. Perhaps unsurprisingly, the U.S., where generated fees are higher, is seen as a key launch market for the startup.
 “Interchange fees in the U.S. are significantly higher than in the EU so this presents a sufficient revenue opportunity for us to perform our reforestation investments and cover marketing and management costs,” explains Cox. “In the EU we’re going to be partnering with an existing retail bank who will provide all our banking infrastructure for free. This will mean that, even though our interchange fee cut is lower, it will be sufficient to cover our costs in the EU. We will announce the name of the bank shortly”.
 Meanwhile, early backer Ecosia is described by the TreeCard founder as its “mother” company. “They’re our closest partner and we’ll be working very closely with them as we grow,” Cox says. “They invested the first cheque into the company and will be doing all our tree planting for us. Ecosia’s marketing team is extremely experienced and they will be helping us use their search engine as a core channel for user acquisition over the next few years”.
 Comments Tom Mendoza, deal partner at EQT Ventures: “TreeCard has the potential to become a leading green finance brand, going where no brand has gone before in creating a de facto platform for impactful financial management. At EQT Ventures, we’re increasingly aware of the environment and the impact that our investments have on the world around us, so we’re really excited to support the TreeCard team, who are actively working with the financial system to create a better future for the planet”.

note:
有关基于编码语言，位置，职称等的开发人员薪水的内容已经涉及很多。但是，多年来没有做任何工作来绘制开发人员薪水涨跌的图表。
 追踪过去20年中开发人员的薪水是一项有趣的工作。研究这个话题意味着改变我用来搜索数据的语言。早在2000年代初期，技术和软件被称为“信息技术”（IT）或“信息和通信技术”（ICT），开发人员仍被称为“ IT工作者”，而关于许多专业角色的提及很少我们今天有。
 时光倒流，我主要参考美国劳工统计局发布的数据，因为它们提供了最全面的年度数据。我查看了从2001年到2019年的隔年数据。
  在世纪之交，CNN Money报告说，入门级计算机程序员的平均起薪为40,800美元。
   由于缺乏进入技术领域的熟练工人，IT毕业生在美国的薪水变得越来越有竞争力。 2000年的薪水已经比上一年提高了10％，除了信息科学专业的学生，​​还远远高于全国平均起薪39,824美元。
  下表显示了2000年所有行业中开发人员，程序员和其他与IT相关的工作职能的平均年薪：
    下图显示了2001年至2011年的年平均工资和平均工资。这些是我根据劳工统计局提供的数据检查的相关工作职能： 
显而易见的是，所有行业中各种职务的平均工资与平均工资之间存在巨大差异，这表明不同部门的IT工人的工资差异很大。
   仅在头十年，美国人就经历了两次巨大的经济危机，在整个美国和全球劳动力市场上产生了涟漪效应。
 2001年是互联网泡沫破灭的一年。那年，尽管网络泡沫破灭，工资并未下降，但到2001年，约有40万从事与IT相关的工作的美国人失去了工作。
 2008年是股市崩盘的时候。与2001年互联网泡沫破灭类似，2008年的工资保持相对不变。但是，到2009年，大约有111,000名从事IT相关领域工作的美国人失去了工作。
 与往年相比，2008年也是工资开始缓慢增长的一年。个人以前每两年平均增加5,000美元，而在2008年之后，工资每两年仅增加2,000美元。
 一般来说，从2001年到2011年，从事技术工作的人的工资随着时间的推移至少增长了21％。系统软件工程师的工资涨幅最大，增长了35％。
    从2013年起，劳动统计局改变了对与技术相关的工作职能进行分类的方式。它在“计算机”类别中增加了更多的工作职能，以反映该专业自2000年代初以来的发展。 
有鉴于此，我分析了可用的广泛工作职能组，而不是深入研究特定的工作职能，以全面了解薪资。
     2013年，美国全国毕业生的工资为45,327美元。科技工作者的收入继续远远高于全国平均水平。
 与上一时期相似，计算机和信息研究人员继续从该组中赚取最多。 2019年，计算机科学家的平均年收入为127,460美元，比软件开发人员和程序员高19％。
  在分析工资时，重要的是要查看职务职能的就业人数，以了解劳动力市场中特定技能的供求情况。
   在20年中，我们可以看到在美国担任计算机编程职位的人数下降了60％。另一方面，同期的年薪增长了47％。
  在本部分中，我删除了2019年的数据，因为我无法分离开发人员功能，无论他们是专门针对应用程序还是系统。
   从2001年到2017年，雇用从事应用程序工作的软件开发人员数量增加了135％。在此期间，年薪增长了47％。 
同期，系统开发人员仅增长了50％，平均工资也增长了50％。
   对于网络开发人员，我们可以看到，在短短的七年中，受雇的专业人员总数增加了31％。该工作职能的平均工资也增加了21％。
  查看数据，我们可以看到开发人员的薪水没有以他们以前的速度增加。从2013年到2019年，开发人员和程序员的年平均工资从92,820美元增加至106,980美元，与前十年的21％的惊人增长相比仅增加了15％。
   增长率下降的原因之一是该数据未显示基于资历和经验的工资。如今，与2000年代初相比，有更多的入门级和初级职位可用，并且有更多的人能够担任这些职位。
 有趣的是，在2015年，Stack Overflow报告称有42％的开发人员表示他们是自学的，而在2019年，有63％的开发人员表示他们主修计算机科学，计算机工程或软件工程，其中86％的受访者表示他们已经教过自己接受新的语言，框架或工具，而无需接受正规课程。
 Dice的另一项调查报告说，虽然经验不足两年的开发人员的工资有所下降，但三年后的薪水却有所上升。就像其他工作一样，开发人员的薪水随着积累的经验而增加。
   在未来的几年中，行业专家预计，随着新工具的开发完全消除对编码的需求，低级编码和编程工作将变得过时。以Web开发人员在构建网站方面的角色为例。使用流行的CMS工具（如WordPress和Squarespace），几乎任何人都可以为其业务建立网站，从而减少了对“仅一点前端网络开发”的了解的人。 
随着自动化消除了对“基本”编码工作的需求，将来对于甚至还不存在的角色，将需要新技能或技能组合。 对现场专家和真正的问题解决者的需求将永远不会消失。 关键是要适应和学习新技术。
note_en:
Much has been covered about developer salaries based on coding language, location, job title, and so on. However, little has been done to chart the rise and fall of developer salaries over the years.
 Tracing developer salaries over the last 20 years was an interesting endeavor. Researching the topic meant changing the language I used to search for data.  Back in the early 2000s, tech and software were referred to as “Information Technology” (IT) or “Information and Communications Technology” (ICT), developers were still referred to as “IT workers” and little was mentioned about the many specialized roles we have today.
 To go back in time, I mainly referred to data published by the  U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics as they offered the most comprehensive year on year data. I looked at data from alternate years, from 2001 to 2019.
  At the turn of the century,  CNN Money reported that the average starting salary for an entry-level computer programmer was $40,800.
   Due to the shortage of skilled workers going into tech fields, salaries in America for IT graduates became more and more competitive. Salaries in 2000 were already 10% higher than the previous year and, apart from information science majors, were well above the  national average starting salary of $39,824.
  The following table shows the average annual salary for developers, programmers, and other IT-related job functions, across all industries in the year 2000:
    The graph below shows the annual median and mean salary from 2001 to 2011. These are the relevant job functions I examined, based on the data available from the Bureau of Labor Statistics:
  What is immediately noticeable is the huge difference between the median and mean salaries for various job functions across all industries, indicating a large difference in salaries for IT workers across the different sectors.
   In the first decade alone, Americans experienced two huge economic crises, sending rippling effects throughout the American and global labor market.
 2001 was the year the dot-com bubble burst. That year, while wages did not fall despite the dot-com burst, around 400,000 Americans working in IT-related roles lost their jobs going into 2001.
 2008 was when the stock market crashed. Similar to the dot-com burst in 2001, wages in 2008 remained relatively unchanged. However, roughly 111,000 Americans working in IT-related fields lost their jobs going into 2009.
 2008 was also the year when wages began to increase at a slower rate compared to previous years. While individuals enjoyed an average of $5,000 increase every two years previously, after 2008, wages only increased by $2,000 every two years.
 Generally speaking, from 2001 to 2011, individuals working in tech had at least a 21% increase in wages over time. Systems Software Engineers experienced the largest jump in wages, an increase of 35%.
    From 2013 onwards, the Bureau of Labor Statistics changed how it categorized tech-related job functions. It added more job functions to the Computing category, mirroring how the profession had evolved since the early 2000s.
 In light of this, I analyzed the broad job function groups available, rather than diving into specific job functions, to get a comprehensive overview of salaries.
     In 2013, the  national American wage for graduates was $45,327. Workers in tech continue to earn well above the national average.
 Similar to the previous period, computer and information researchers continued to earn the most out of the group. In 2019, the average annual income for computer scientists was $127,460, 19% ahead of software developers, and programmers.
  When analyzing salaries, it is also important to look at the employment numbers for job functions to get an indication of the supply and demand for specific skills in the labor market.
   In 20 years, we can see a  60% drop in the number of people employed for computer programming roles in America. Annual wages, on the other hand, increased by 47% in the same period.
  In this section, I removed 2019 data as I was unable to separate developer functions, whether they specialized in applications or systems.
   The number of software developers hired to work on applications has increased by 135% from 2001 to 2017. Annual salaries increased by 47% in that period.
   Systems developers increased by only 50% in the same period, with average wages increasing by 50% as well.
   For web developers, we can see that the total number of employed professionals increased by 31% in just seven years. The average wages for this job function also increased by 21%.
  Looking at the data, we can see that developer salaries are not increasing at the rate they used to. As we head into 2013 through to 2019, the annual mean salary for developers and programmers increased from $92,820 to $106,980, a mere 15% increase compared to the whopping 21% increase experienced in the previous decade.
   One of the reasons for the slower rate of increase is that the data does not show wages based on seniority and experience. Today, there are more entry-level and junior roles available compared to the early 2000s, and there are more people able to fill those roles.
 Interestingly, in  2015, Stack Overflow reported that 42% of developers indicated they were self-taught while in  2019, 63% of developers said they majored in computer science, computer engineering, or software engineering, with 86% of respondents reporting to have taught themselves a new language, framework or tool without taking a formal course.
 Another survey by  Dice reported that while wages have dropped for developers with less than two years experience, salaries pick up after the three-year mark. Much like other jobs, developer salaries increase with more experience they accumulate.
   In the years to come, industry experts expect  low-level coding and programming jobs to become obsolete with the development of new tools that remove the need to code completely. Take for example the role of Web Developers when it comes to building websites. With popular CMS tools like WordPress and Squarespace, almost anyone can build a website for their business, reducing the need for people who know “just a little bit of front end web development”.
 As automation takes away the need for “basic” coding jobs, new skills or a combination of skills will be needed in the future for roles that don’t even exist yet. The need for field experts and true problem solvers will never disappear. The key is to adapt and learn as new technologies emerge.
title: 开发人员薪资的演变：回顾20年
title_en: The Evolution of Developer Salaries: Looking Back 20 Years

 斯坦福互联网天文台向Clubhouse通报了安全漏洞，并于2月12日表示正与该应用程序公司合作以提高其安全性。
  听到可以将Clubhouse上的音频对话从应用程序中删除的声音可能会令人震惊，但这并不是什么新鲜事。
 用户已经在其设备上使用视频和音频录制功能来捕获Elon Musk和Kevin Hart等名人的对话，并将其上传到YouTube。 
再次，这违反了该应用程序的服务条款，但这确实意味着没有人期望他们的对话实际上是私人的，Thiel先生警告说。

 他补充说，消费者需要对服务如何处理其数据持现实态度。

note_en:
Audio-only social network Clubhouse has confirmed that a user managed to stream content from the app on their website.
 The app allows users to participate in public or private audio chatrooms, promising that the content has to be experienced live and is not recorded.
 US cyber-security researchers said a user found a way to stream feeds from multiple chatrooms on Sunday.
 Clubhouse confirmed the spill, which is when data is released to a place that is not authorised to have access to it.
 The firm told Bloomberg it had banned the user and installed new "safeguards" to prevent conversations from being streamed again.
 Clubhouse told the BBC that recording or streaming without the explicit permission of the speakers violates the app's terms and conditions.
  A spokeswoman said: "Over the weekend, an individual temporarily streamed multiple rooms from their own feed to a website.
 "This individual's account has been permanently banned from the service and we have added additional safeguards to prevent people from doing this in the future."
  Stanford University's Internet Observatory reported the incident first, but the programme's chief technology officer David Thiel stressed that the data spill was not malicious or a "hack". Instead, he said that it seemed a user had decided to violate Clubhouse's terms.
  He explained that a "data spillage" was different to a "data breach", in that data breaches are deliberate and usually carried out by someone hacking into a system to steal data.
 A data spillage, on the other hand, is an incident whereby confidential information is released into an environment that is not authorised to have access to the information.
   According to him, the incident occurred because a user had realised that it was possible to be in multiple chatrooms at once.
 By understanding how this worked, the user could connect a Clubhouse API to his website, and essentially "share" his login remotely with anyone on the internet who wanted to listen to the audio chats from the app.
 "If you're popular, people will make a third-party app that scrapes data from the service, for example all the third-party programs that scrape information from Twitter," Mr Potter told the BBC.
  Sunday's incident comes after Clubhouse made assurances that user data couldn't be stolen by cyber-criminals or state-sponsored hackers, in response to a warning from Stanford University's Internet Observatory, which is headed by Facebook's former security chief Alex Stamos.
 Stanford's cyber-security researchers  discovered several security flaws, including the fact that the users' unique ID numbers and the ID numbers of the Clubhouse chatrooms they created were being transmitted in plaintext and it could be possible connect IDs to specific user profiles.
 The researchers were also concerned that the Chinese government could gain access to the raw audio files on Clubhouse's servers, because its back-end infrastructure is provided by a real-time engagement API firm called Agora, which has offices in both Shanghai and San Francisco.
  When Agora went public on Wall Street in June, it mentioned  in its filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) that in China it would be required "to provide assistance and support in accordance with the law for public security and national security authorities to protect national security or assist with criminal investigations".
 Stanford Internet Observatory informed Clubhouse about the security flaws and on 12 February said that it was working with the app firm to improve its security.
  While it might sound alarming to hear that audio conversations on Clubhouse can be taken out of the app, this isn't exactly new.
 Users are already using the video and audio recording functions on their devices to capture conversations had by celebrities like Elon Musk and Kevin Hart, and uploading them to YouTube.
  Again, this is against the app's terms of service, but it does mean that no-one should expect their conversations to actually be private, warns Mr Thiel.
 "Consider Clubhouse chats to be semi-public, given issues with Agora and the fact we all have microphones,"  he tweeted.
 Mr Potter thinks the problem is more that Clubhouse is young and still immature as a service.
 "I feel like there's a bunch of users who got really enthusiastic because it's a new thing and because you need an invitation, the conversations must be private," he said.
 "It happened with Zoom and Tiktok - again and again, we see an app that has really high growth, it goes viral, and then they have a privacy problem, or they find lots of problems that weren't so big a deal when they were smaller, and cyber-security comes later."
 He added that consumers needed to be realistic about what services do with their data.
 "I think people just need to realise that the privacy and cyber-security of newer social media platforms isn't going to be as good as mature ones," said Mr Potter.
 "If you're going to be an early adopter and try out new apps and new smartphones, there's going to be bugs."
title: 会所确认其音频流的数据泄漏
title_en: Clubhouse confirms data spillage of its audio streams
